textPlain: Make sure to read the post linked right at the beginning as well: http://0x80.pl/notesen/2023-04-30-lookup-in-strings.html as well as the magic bitboards linked, too https://www.chessprogramming.org/Magic_BitboardsThough honestly this post really needed some numbers and benchmarks.
What's most annoying with gperf and similar tools is that they aren't really suited to applications where the set of keys is known at runtime during initialization.

I never really finished the project, thus only the rough qualitative benchmarks you get at the bottom (measured mostly by profiling and size(1)); I saw that it wasn't enough of a win in the larger context where I needed it, thus it made sense to stop early instead of making exhaustive benchmarks.The blog post was mainly for curious readers, I'm surprised it hit HN at all. :-)
